title: Archived outbreak investigations: Public Health Notices - Canada.ca
content:
## Archived outbreak investigations
Links to closed outbreak investigations are provided here for information and reference purposes. For active investigations, please refer to the Public Health Notices .
- March 19, 2026 (Original Notice: December 22,2025) Outbreak of E. coli infections linked to Pillsbury brand Pizza Pops
- January 30, 2026 (Original notice: September 15, 2025) Outbreak of Salmonella infections linked to dog food and treats
- September 16, 2025 (Original notice: June 11, 2025) Outbreak of Salmonella infections linked to various brands of salami and cacciatore products
- March 19, 2025 (Original notice January 19, 2025) Outbreak of Salmonella infections linked to imported pastries
- October 25, 2024 (Original Notice: September 27, 2024) Outbreak of Salmonella Muenchen infections linked to geckos
- October 11, 2024 (Original Notice: July 17, 2024) Outbreak of Listeria infections linked to recalled plant-based refrigerated beverages
- May 14, 2024 (Original Notice: March 22, 2024) Outbreak of Salmonella infections linked to geckos
- May 14, 2024 (Original Notice: April 13, 2023) Outbreak of Salmonella infections linked to snakes and rodents
- January 29, 2024 (Original Notice: November 17, 2023) Outbreak of Salmonella infections linked to Malichita and Rudy brand cantaloupes
- March 28, 2024 (Original Notice: November 11, 2023) Outbreak of extensively drug-resistant Salmonella infections linked to raw pet food and contact with cattle
- October 4, 2023 (Original Notice: September 14, 2023) Outbreak of botulism in Bordeaux, France linked to sardines
- October 16, 2023 (Original notice: June 23, 2023) Investigation into non-travel related Cyclospora infections Each spring and summer, Canada sees an increase in non-travel related Cyclospora illnesses reported to the Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC). Between May and September 2023, 398 cases were reported to PHAC occurring in Ontario (328), Quebec (46), British Columbia (21), Newfoundland and Labrador (2) and Nova Scotia (1). The source or sources of illnesses were not identified. PHAC continues to work with its public health and food safety partners to identify possible ways Cyclospora infections occur in Canada each year.

- October 14, 2022 (Original Notice: July 30, 2022) Investigation into non-travel related Cyclospora infections Each spring and summer, Canada sees an increase in non-travel related Cyclospora illnesses illnesses reported to the Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC). Between May and September 2022, 325 cases were reported to PHAC occurring in Ontario (260), Quebec (57), British Columbia (4), Newfoundland and Labrador (3) and Alberta (1). The source or sources of illnesses were not identified. PHAC continues to work with its public health and food safety partners to identify possible ways Cyclospora infections occur in Canada each year.

- July 18, 2022 (Original Notice: June 2, 2022) Outbreak of hepatitis A infections linked to imported fresh organic strawberries

- October 14, 2021 (Original Notice: July 30, 2021) Investigation into non-travel related Cyclospora infections Each spring and summer, Canada sees an increase in non-travel related Cyclospora illnesses reported to the Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC). Between May and September 2021, 75 cases were reported to PHAC occurring in Ontario (60), Quebec (10), British Columbia (3), and Newfoundland and Labrador (2). The source or sources of illnesses were not identified. PHAC continues to work with its public health and food safety partners to identify possible ways Cyclospora infections occur in Canada each year.
